programmes.show_description: Restaurants: Sandi Toksvig invites us to join her in the QI restaurant with Alan Davies, Phill Jupitus, Sara Pascoe and Mark Watson.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Stephen Mangan is the host as Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey and comedian Rachel Parris join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by Bob Mortimer, Samantha Morton, Miles Jupp and Sarah Hadland.
Claudia Winkleman, the Reverend Kate Bottley, Sara Barron and Ed Gamble join Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell for the award-winning panel show.
Aliens take over the world but struggle to govern a species as baffling as humans, so they hire a 'Human Liaison' in the UK: Stewart, a hapless twentysomething from the local council.
Stewart's plan to boost alien popularity via social media goes awry - in doing so, he attracts attention from anti-alien resistance fighters who tap into Stewart as a potential mole.
Stewart tries to broker peace between humans and aliens, but as Grieves goes rogue, Stewart finds the future of the entire planet is at stake... and it's all resting on his shoulders.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country and also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife.
Preston: Sean Kelly presides over more cut-throat auction antics. Heavy D's gamble might just leave him with the hump. And has Jesse got what it takes to win big in the UK?
Lewes: Expect lots of fireworks as the always entertaining reality show heads to the UK! A vehicle sends bids sky high. John has spotted treasure. Scotty and Dan play dirty.
An elderly couple arrives from Mexico with five suitcases but no clothes, and a truck carrying garden furniture has something extra in the load.
Officers pull over a man from Mexico who is making a rare journey to the US in a new car and a pedestrian gets more than he bargains for when he is mistaken for a wanted man.
Freddie samples the brutal full-contact motorsport of F1 stock cars, Chris gets to grips with Mercedes' 'F1 car for the road', and Paddy takes a drive in a luxurious Range Rover.
Chris and Freddie check out a new hot hatch from Honda and an old hot hatch from Peugeot, and Paddy tries to get a team of young amateur racers up to professional standards - fast!
Paul and Bob return to Scotland to fish for salmon once more, this time on the mighty River Tweed after a previous last-ditch attempt to catch the fish on the River Tay.
Paul takes Bob on a trip down memory lane and back to the River Lea in Hertfordshire - where Paul used to come as a teenager with his mates - and welcomes a special guest.
Cheese: The bikers explore how Britain's love affair with cheese has evolved over centuries, and discover that the original cheesecake recipes were British.
Feel Good Food: Uplifting stuff as the bikers explore how certain recipes can put a spring back in your step, whether it's a bowl of chicken noodle soup or a fish dish.
Robson and actor Mark Benton go on a humour-filled quest to find ways to relax while exploring the North Yorkshire coast.
Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country and also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife.
Congo: Simon Reeve explores the Democratic Republic of the Congo, encountering thick jungles and swamps as he looks for those whose home is Africa's greatest rainforest.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Zori: A gunfight in suburban Kent leaves a victim seriously wounded and sparks a frenetic high-pressure race to hunt down the perpetrators.
Fears: It's time for Will and Ralf to face their fears, from dangling on a trapeze and tackling fatherhood to the ultimate horror of performing stand-up in front of a Glasgow crowd.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Stephen Mangan is the host as Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey and comedian Rachel Parris join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Restaurants: Sandi Toksvig invites us to join her in the QI restaurant with Alan Davies, Phill Jupitus, Sara Pascoe and Mark Watson.
Dara O Briain and the team return to bring us the usual hilarious examination of not-so-topical events with guests Katherine Ryan, Rob Beckett, Ed Byrne and Stewart Francis.
TV's funniest comics offer a satirical take on world events, via improv and stand-up. Alistair McGowan, Hal Cruttenden, Gary Delaney and Miles Jupp join Dara O Briain and co.
More cutting-edge stand-up from the iconic theatre in London. The inimitable Larry Dean welcomes comedians Harriet Kemsley and Slim to the stage.
Menagerie: Stephen Fry asks Sue Perkins, Romesh Ranganathan, Bill Bailey and Alan Davies beastly posers on moose, monkeys and moustachioed toads in the highly evolved quiz.
New Car: Motor-based antics with the lovably daft Rose family. Johnny and Moira try to get a deal on a used car, and David helps Stevie put her great aunt to rest.
Driving Test: More putting of the pedal to the metal in the popular sitcom. Johnny and Moira worry about the motel's future, and David panics at the thought of retaking his driving test.
Rooms by the Hour: More laugh-out-loud comedy with the lovable Rose family. Johnny imperils the motel when he rents rooms to a dubious enterprise, and David helps Moira with an audition.
Murder Mystery: Another visit to the small town with some big hitters. Johnny and Stevie pitch a deal to a nearby golf course, and Moira tries to get out of Twyla's murder mystery party.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Foraging: The bikers continue their journey through time, celebrating British food. In this episode, they take to the undergrowth in search of delicious free food.
Officers are on the lookout for day-trippers trying to smuggle prescription pills into America, bought cheaply from one of the 200 pharmacies on the other side of the border.
Suspicions are raised when a routine x-ray reveals suspicious packages hidden inside the tyres of a pick-up truck. Officers also seize a large shipment of marijuana.
Documentary series. Bernie's odyssey goes horribly wrong, two veteran locomotives risk destruction in a race for glory, and a mega-train faces the steepest grades in Australia.
Thailand and Malaysia: More colourful eastern adventures with Rick Stein. In Thailand, he learns the art of hot and sour tom yum goong, and in Melaka he enjoys Nyonya cuisine.
In Pharr 840kg of meth is discovered in the floor of a truck. Aromatic candles filled with Fentanyl are intercepted at the World Trade Bridge.
Two 16-year-olds are suspected of smuggling drugs for a cartel. A man tries to enter the US with false documents. Marijuana worth $7million is found inside rolls of carpet.
Trawlerman: In one of the most intense experiences of his life, Grimsby-based Guy Martin takes on the dangerous job of working as a deckhand on a fishing trawler.
James May tries to drive a Toyota up an active volcano and the boys host a star-studded launch party for their Reasonably Priced Car. With guests Al Murray and Bill Bailey.
Motoring mayhem. The boys test out some old bangers in Germany, and former Labour PR chief Alastair Campbell takes a Reasonably Priced Car for a 'spin'.
Foraging: The bikers continue their journey through time, celebrating British food. In this episode, they take to the undergrowth in search of delicious free food.
Thailand and Malaysia: More colourful eastern adventures with Rick Stein. In Thailand, he learns the art of hot and sour tom yum goong, and in Melaka he enjoys Nyonya cuisine.
At Christmas: Rob Brydon hosts a special Christmas episode as guests Jo Brand, Joe Swash, Joe Lycett and Ruth Madeley join Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort festive facts from fibs.
Retired footballer David James, comedians Rhys James and Isy Suttie, and athletics legend Denise Lewis tackle the best-selling author's fun-filled brainteasers in this edition.
More mind-bending puzzles and fun with the ever-genial Pointless personality - he's joined this time by Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams.
Menagerie: Stephen Fry asks Sue Perkins, Romesh Ranganathan, Bill Bailey and Alan Davies beastly posers on moose, monkeys and moustachioed toads in the highly evolved quiz.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists for a compilation that looks back at the big news of 2024.
